_meta.hint: lc quality 60<80; conv words 68-105: 3/5 — R1 items 117 and 122 words ran long. These 3 supplements keep every conversation strictly within 68-105 words.

items.situation: a student asking gym front-desk staff about replacing a lost locker key

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I think I lost the key to locker forty-two. Can I get a new one?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, no worries. We can cut a replacement, but there's a small five-dollar fee.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's fine. Do you need anything from me first?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Just your student ID so I can confirm the locker is yours.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Here it is. How long will the new key take?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: About ten minutes. Grab a seat and I'll call you when it's ready.

questions.stem: Why does the woman approach the man?

options.A: To get a replacement for a lost locker key
options.B: To complain that her locker will not open
options.C: To sign up for a new gym membership
options.D: To report that her ID card was stolen

questions.explanation: 女生一开口就说丢了locker forty-two的钥匙并想要新的，所以A正确。B她没说锁打不开，而是钥匙丢了；C她不是来办会员；D丢的是钥匙不是ID，都是干扰项。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Leave the gym to find her old key
options.B: Wait nearby until her new key is ready
options.C: Pay a monthly fee for a bigger locker
options.D: Ask a friend to lend her a key

questions.explanation: 男生让她坐下等，大约十分钟做好后会叫她，所以她最可能在附近等待，B正确。A她已经放弃找旧钥匙；C没人提到月费或更大柜子；D对话里没有朋友借钥匙的情节。

items.situation: two students deciding whether to attend a second guest lecture in a campus series

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Did you make it to the climate talk last night? It was packed.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I did! The speaker was great, but I couldn't see a single slide from the back.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Same here. Apparently there's a second talk Thursday in the bigger auditorium.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's good. The seats there are tiered, so the view should be much better.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. Want to go together and grab seats early this time?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Definitely. Let's meet at the entrance around six-thirty.

questions.stem: What problem did the woman have at the first talk?

options.A: She arrived after the lecture had started
options.B: She could not see the slides from the back
options.C: She found the speaker difficult to follow
options.D: She could not find an empty seat at all

questions.explanation: 女生说演讲很好，但从后排一张幻灯片都看不到，所以B正确。A她没说迟到；C她说speaker很棒，不是难懂；D她坐在后排，说明有座位，只是看不清，都是干扰项。

questions.stem: Why do the speakers expect the second talk to be better?

options.A: The auditorium has tiered seating with clearer views
options.B: The same speaker will use larger slides
options.C: Fewer students are expected to attend
options.D: The talk will be recorded for later viewing

questions.explanation: 女生指出第二场在更大的礼堂，座位是阶梯式的，视野会好很多，所以A正确。B没人提幻灯片变大；C没说人会变少；D没提录像，都是无依据的推断。

items.situation: a student asking the IT help desk about resetting a locked campus portal password

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'm locked out of the campus portal. I tried my password too many times.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: No problem. For security, the account locks after five failed attempts. I can reset it for you.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Great. Do I need to come back later, or can you do it now?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Right now. I'll send a reset link to your student email in a minute.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. Should I change anything else once I'm back in?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Just set up the backup phone number so you can reset it yourself next time.

questions.stem: Why is the woman locked out of the portal?

options.A: She entered her password too many times
options.B: Her student account has already expired
options.D: The portal was down for maintenance

questions.explanation: 女生说密码试了太多次被锁，男生证实失败五次后账户会锁定，所以A正确。B没提账户过期；C她忘的是密码不是用户名；D不是系统维护，都是干扰项。

questions.stem: What does the man suggest the woman do to avoid this problem in the future?

options.A: Memorize a longer and stronger password
options.B: Set up a backup phone number for self-reset
options.C: Visit the help desk once every semester
options.D: Log in only from campus computers

questions.explanation: 男生建议她设置备用手机号，以后就能自己重置密码，所以B正确。A没提更强的密码；C没说每学期来一次；D没限制只能用校内电脑，都是无依据的干扰项。
